opp.ais_why:
• The products on this deal are iSaaS, Agentic AI, and ANA, and Zycus has flagged AI as Very Critical for the account. The 5/4/2026 next-step note records the McAfee ANA POC launched on 5/5. On the 5/5/2025 Avoma call, the buyer confirmed direct interest in Zycus's autonomous negotiation and spend dynamics capabilities.
• The 1/19/2026 rep note captures McAfee wanting to see whether the vendor can deliver on its Agentic AI innovation claims. The 10/21/2025 note documents Aitor pushing on integrating Zycus's autonomous negotiation with Omnia for indirect tail-spend orchestration, with detailed back-and-forth on AI accuracy, hallucination prevention, benchmark confidence, and human-in-the-loop design.
• The buyer's own POC scope requires an evidence-based business case validated through real-world use cases, plus scalability and operational viability post-POC across categories. McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3 explicitly for the AI and ANA evaluation, which is enterprise-grade commitment, not curiosity.

items.quote: McAfee meeting w CFO on Monday

items.area: Peer POC vendor(s) in the final 3 never named
items.quote: one or two vendors, and see the results

items.area: No POC use case completed end-to-end; McAfee-specific savings not yet quantified
items.quote: we are now relaunching new use cases... simulating with test suppliers in order to accelerate

metrics.narrative: McAfee's objective is to stop skilled buyers manually negotiating low-value indirect deals: Mazen El-Haidari (21 October 2025) framed the waste as buyers 'not negotiating for 3 weeks to save $22,000 on a contract', and the buyer wants to push the autonomous threshold from roughly $50,000 toward $100,000. Quantified impact so far is vendor-supplied (Zycus early-adopter benchmarks of 4-6% tail-spend savings and 70-80% negotiation-time reduction, Devika, 21 October 2025); McAfee has not yet quantified its own baseline. The POC has not produced a completed end-to-end event, so the McAfee-specific savings number remains uncaptured — building it is the gating move.

champion.narrative: Aitor Gonzalez Martin is the active, agenda-driving champion — he initiated the outreach, scopes each session and drives the POC (17 October 2025 onward, engaged through 2 July 2026). Mazen El-Haidari (procurement excellence, Houston) is a supportive second thread with executive visibility via the CPONext/Horizon feature (12 June 2026). The gap: neither has a proven bridge to the budget owner, so the relationship is concentrated on two mid-level transformation owners; champion strength is developing — strong on the evaluation, unproven on power.

competition.narrative: The strongest current threat is the do-nothing / build-on-existing-stack path (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration, freshly deployed) combined with genuine AI-readiness skepticism — until ANA completes a use case end-to-end, the status quo wins by default (17-21 October 2025; reinforced by the 24 June 2026 supplier-responsiveness concern). McAfee tightened the shortlist from 9 vendors to 3, and one or two peer POC vendors remain but have never been named (Aitor, 17 October 2025: 'one or two vendors') — a real knowledge gap. Zycus's wedge is unified source-to-pay scalability, native Teams intake and early-adopter agentic-negotiation ROI, activated by winning the relaunched POC on McAfee's own data.

identify_pain.narrative: McAfee underwent an ~18-month transformation (SAP to Workday, Omnia orchestration) and now wants to automate low-value tactical and tailspend sourcing so skilled buyers stop spending weeks on small negotiations (Mazen El-Haidari and Aitor, 17-21 October 2025). Aitor owns the pain as the evaluation lead and is explicit that services/indirect coverage — not just tailspend — is critical ('that's what we most spend... that's critical for us', 21 October 2025). Pain is real, owner-attributed and reaffirmed through the POC.

paper_process.narrative: An NDA was signed (Aitor referenced it on the 21 October 2025 call), and a POC agreement was routed for signature in April 2026 via Alejandro Lanzavecchia. Commercial structure was outlined verbally — spend-tier ANA licensing with a separate integration scope of work (Alexa Bradley, 21 October 2025) — but no written commercial proposal, MSA/SOW, or InfoSec review is yet in motion. The path from POC success to signature is not formalized and no paper owner is named; there is no contract-expiration forcing function.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged. A CFO meeting was referenced once (Next Step 11 June 2026: 'McAfee meeting w CFO on Monday') with no readout, and an internal CEO/CIO review of the ANA video was set for the week of 24 June 2026 — both are validation touchpoints, not confirmed budget sponsorship. Nick Priest (Head of Global Procurement) is the most senior mapped buyer and receives updates, but budget authority above him is unmapped; Alexa Bradley's planned executive note to Nick (Next Step 19 June 2026) is the current bridge to power. Naming and engaging the CFO is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call and activity sequence: McAfee ran discovery (17 October 2025), a capability demo (21 October 2025), signed a POC agreement (April 2026), and is now in a weekly-cadence POC with reference-customer checks (Nippon Gas 18 June 2026) and an internal CEO/CIO video review (week of 24 June 2026). The buyer stated it wants to finalize the initial use case within 1-2 weeks, then test two additional use cases before a go/no-go (Next Step 11 June 2026). The executive go/no-go session remains undated ('executive session w leadership - TBD') and the approval chain above procurement is not documented.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is an evidence-based POC: McAfee's own scope requires a business case validated through real-world use cases plus scalability and operational viability post-POC across categories (AIS rationale; POC agreement April 2026). Aitor repeatedly named the criteria that matter most — coverage of indirect and tactical/intangible services rather than just tailspend, autonomous-negotiation accuracy with human-in-the-loop, and benchmark-based target pricing over McAfee's internal data (Aitor, 21 October 2025: 'I prefer for Anna to be using the benchmark'). Supplier responsiveness within live events has since become an explicit pass/fail criterion (Aitor email 24 June 2026).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 59.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Aitor Gonzalez Martin is an active, agenda-driving champion with Mazen El-Haidari a supportive second thread. Developed: strong advocacy and two-way engagement through 2 July 2026. Missing: a proven bridge to the economic buyer and budget sponsorship.

bullets.text: 31 July close not credible; 0 of 5 POC use cases completed end-to-end and second close-date slip in a month
Economic buyer (CFO) unmapped and unengaged; executive go/no-go session undated
ANA-only commercial proposal (spend-tier licensing + separate integration range) still not delivered in writing; buyer gated continuation on commercials aligning
Supplier responsiveness flagged as a potential structural limitation; intangible/complex-services accuracy still under development

Facilitate the Tata Play reference call, the buyer's stated number-one choice. (by 10 Jul)
Complete one POC use case end-to-end and quantify McAfee's savings and cycle-time gain. (by 16 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 59 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); momentum 64 vs stage-expected 56. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 64 (moving forward): Next Step carries 3 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 42 is anchored on the stage win (59), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 64, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 9.0 / Strong, corroborated by the calls: McAfee narrowed 9 vendors to 3 specifically for the AI/ANA evaluation and runs a live autonomous-negotiation POC. The field is if anything under-rated by nothing — the calls confirm genuine agentic appetite.

business_case.evidence: Pain is articulated with target thresholds ($22k/3-week waste, $50k-100k autonomy bands) and vendor benchmarks (4-6% tail-spend savings, 70-80% negotiation-time reduction), but McAfee has not quantified its own baseline and no POC use case has completed to produce a McAfee-specific ROI number.

Zycus provided detailed written responses to Aitor's POC feedback points
Nippon Gas reference call held with Tania Corral (buyer reported she spoke highly of Zycus)
Owner changed Kevin Cipollaro -> Karson Keogh

reasons.detail: This $200K Best Case (forecasted) opportunity had its close date pushed from Jun 30 to Jul 31 on 29 June, and the ANA POC still shows 0 of 5 use cases completed with supplier-responsiveness flagged as a structural gap. No economic buyer is mapped (only Nick Priest, Head of Global Procurement, is engaged; the CFO is unnamed), so there is no confirmed budget sponsor behind the slip. If the quarter's forecast leans on McAfee, a stalled POC plus an unproven CFO means the deal likely pushes again and the status-quo Workday/Omnia stack wins by default.
reasons.metric: Close date pushed 31 days (Jun 30 -> Jul 31); POC 0 of 5 use cases
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Karson why McAfee's close date slipped to Jul 31, whether any of the 5 POC use cases will land before month-end, and who the real economic buyer/CFO is behind a forecasted $200K deal.
reasons.summary: Forecasted McAfee deal pushed close date a month (Jun 30 -> Jul 31), POC 0 of 5 proven
reasons.evidence: CloseDate changed 2026-06-30 -> 2026-07-31 by Karson Keogh on 2026-06-29; competitive_position: '0 of 5 POC use cases completed... status quo wins by default until ANA proves out'.

critical_signals.text: The real threat is the status quo: with no POC use case yet completed end-to-end, McAfee's existing Workday plus Omnia stack wins by default until ANA proves out.
After the 18 June Nippon Gas reference call the buyer said she spoke highly of Zycus but raised follow-up concerns, and the buyer's number-one reference, Tata Play, is still pending.
Aitor flagged supplier responsiveness in the live events as a potential structural limitation on 24 June, prompting new test-supplier use cases and shipped WhatsApp/SMS/email reminders.
The ANA-only commercial proposal Aitor asked for is still undelivered and the CFO who owns budget has been referenced only once with no readout.
A CEO/CIO internal review of the ANA video was set for the week of 24 June, but neither has joined a call and both remain unengaged with Zycus directly.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and two-way through early July, but momentum is process-heavy (references, feedback loops) rather than commercial, and the decision gate has no date.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 6 May 2025, ~14 months in cycle, in Shortlisted. Close 31 July 2026 = 28 days out (swept 3 July 2026). Deal is LIVE: weekly POC follow-ups scheduled through 23 July, buyer emails through 2 July. But the 31 July date is not credible: 0 of 5 POC use cases have completed end-to-end, the ANA-only commercial proposal is undelivered, the economic buyer (CFO) is unmapped, and the executive go/no-go session is undated. Reference track is mid-flight (Nippon Gas done 18 June; Tata Play, the buyer's #1, pending). A forward slip of ~60 days is required.
evidence: Aitor Gonzalez Martin (McAfee) email 24 June 2026: supplier responsiveness in the live POC is a potential 'structural limitation' and new use cases are being relaunched with test suppliers to accelerate results — no use case has completed end-to-end yet.

Next Step 11 June 2026: 'McAfee has an internal meeting planned with CEO/CIO for week of june 24 to review the ANA video... finalize evaluation of this initial use case within the next 1-2 weeks' — validation gate, not a commercial or budget approval.
Next Step 4 June 2026: 'executive session w leadership - TBD' — the decision gate has no date.
Field history 29 June 2026: CloseDate pushed 30 June -> 31 July by Karson Keogh — second slip in a month (previously 4 June -> 30 June).
Weekly ANA PoC follow-up events scheduled 9, 16 and 23 July 2026 plus buyer email 2 July 2026 confirm the deal is engaged and live.
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, genuinely engaged POC with a strong AI-fit signal, but the 31 July close cannot hold: no use case has completed end-to-end, the ANA commercial proposal and the economic buyer are still open, and supplier responsiveness was flagged as a potential structural limitation. The deal is good; the date is optimistic (realistic close ~September 2026).

flags: Convert the ANA-video review into a written go/no-go and secure a dated executive session — the decision gate is still 'TBD'.
Reach the economic buyer (CFO): Alexa Bradley's executive note to Nick Priest is the bridge — the budget owner is unmapped and unengaged, blocking the commercial close.

Complete at least one POC use case end-to-end with McAfee's own savings numbers — 0 of 5 done and supplier responsiveness flagged as a structural limitation, so the do-nothing path wins by default until ANA proves out.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most right now: lock the executive decision gate, reach the budget owner, and make the POC produce a quantified end-to-end result.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer runs an active ANA POC, requested customer references and an ANA commercial proposal, and said it would 'deploy ASAP' if the POC produces a successful business case (Aitor, 21 October 2025 and June 2026) — decisive Zycus-specific next steps, though contingent on POC results.

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the single strongest CURRENT threat is the do-nothing / status-quo path (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration): with 0 of 5 POC use cases completed and supplier responsiveness flagged as a potential structural limitation on 24 June 2026, the status quo wins by default until ANA proves out. One or two peer POC vendors remain in the final 3 but are still unnamed (17 October 2025) — a knowledge gap that blocks tailored positioning. The Workday/Omnia stack is also the incumbent Zycus must integrate with, not replace. PACTEM is a low post-sourcing adjacency; Zip is dormant since May 2025.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / existing Workday + Omnia stack
competitors.quote: are we ready for this kind of solution? Is our procurement function ready?
competitors.how_we_win: Win the relaunched POC on McAfee's own data: close the supplier-responsiveness gap (WhatsApp/SMS/email reminders shipped 29 June), complete at least one use case end-to-end, and quantify savings and throughput so the ROI beats status quo.

competitors.name: Unnamed peer POC vendor(s) in the final 3
competitors.quote: one or two vendors, and see the results
competitors.how_we_win: Get Aitor or Nick to name the peer, then differentiate on full source-to-pay scalability and native Teams intake vs a point tool; surface it live in the POC head-to-head.

competitors.name: Incumbent stack (Workday sourcing + Omnia orchestration)
competitors.quote: we're using Omnia as an intake for purchases... that logic will remain in Omnia
competitors.how_we_win: Position ANA as an overlay that integrates to Omnia/Workday (single integration point, lower implementation cost), not a rip-and-replace.

competitors.name: Zip
competitors.quote: we're looking at things in the market like Zip, like Zycus
competitors.how_we_win: Not referenced since May 2025; monitor only — emphasize unified platform vs orchestration-only point tools.

Strongly positioned on the AI story; the exposure is intangible-services accuracy, not the AI narrative itself.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: McAfee expects autonomous negotiation to cover indirect and tactical/intangible services (not just tailspend) with human-in-the-loop, benchmark-based pricing, and provable savings on their own data. Zycus is aligned on tailspend and quotable services but exposed on complex/intangible services accuracy, which is still under development.
